Request For Entry Of Default Judgment
Off Calendar May-16-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default
Off Calendar May-14-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Anthony Obidiagha's motion to vacate the default entered against him on February 28, 2018 is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on the merits and there is no discernable legal prejudice to plaintiff BE & JDS, Inc. by the grant of the motion. Mr. Obidiagha has shown that he is entitled to have the default set aside per the mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Moreover, the default was taken on the original complaint filed even though plaintiff had filed an amended complaint on January 23, 2018, which superseded the original, and plaintiff was required to serve the amended complaint in the same manner as the summons before entry of default because defendant had yet to appear in the case. The objections to the Emeziem and Obidiagha declarations are overruled. While the declarations refer to "information and belief," they also are based on personal knowledge. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
